pre_close_setups.approach: Document the current pain, then contrast
pre_close_setups.verbatim: So just to summarise what you've told me — [current provider/situation] has been costing you [specific loss/frustration]. Here's what that looks like if we can fix it.
The difference between where you are now and where you could be is [£X/year]. The question is just timing.

offer_framings.verbatim: I want to make it easy for you to say yes — so I'm going to offer you 13% instead of our standard 15%. That's locked in for the life of the contract. If you can confirm by [specific date], I'll get the agreement over today.
I'll match the rate on your [existing property] — 13% across both. Makes the decision simple.

meeting_2_asks.verbatim: Let's get something in the diary for [specific time] — even just 20 minutes. By then you'll have had a chance to look at the agreement and we can go through any questions.
I'm going to send you the action plan and agreement today. Can we speak again next [day] to go through it?

recovery_when_not_ready.situation: Delay signal is present (tenant, purchase, renovation, relocation)
recovery_when_not_ready.verbatim_response: Completely understand — you need [X] to resolve first. Let me get the action plan over to you so everything is ready to move the moment that clears. And I'll put a note in the diary to call you in [N] weeks.

recovery_when_not_ready.situation: Lead needs to discuss with partner
recovery_when_not_ready.verbatim_response: Of course — this is a joint decision. Send me over any questions you think [partner] might have and I'll answer them in writing so you have something to share. And let's find a time when you can both be on a call.
recovery_when_not_ready.outcome: Surfaces partner objections before they kill the deal

recovery_when_not_ready.situation: Lead is analytical and needs more data
recovery_when_not_ready.verbatim_response: Let me send you a worked example with your property's actual numbers — not percentages, but the specific net figure you'd see on your statement each month. If that works for you, the decision should be straightforward.
recovery_when_not_ready.outcome: Converts analytical objection into a calculation exercise

delay_present_close.close_approach: Don't offer. Instead: 'Let me get the agreement sent so you have it when you're ready — and let's set a date to reconnect [N weeks before tenancy end].'
Don't offer before exchange. After exchange: 'Congratulations — now let's get everything set up so you go live within 3 weeks of getting the keys.'
delay_present_close.what_to_commit_to_instead: Agreement ready to sign on completion day + setup quote

delay_present_close.close_approach: If departure < 60 days: set offer expiry to match departure date. 'You need to decide before you leave — here's the offer valid until [departure minus 7 days].'
delay_present_close.what_to_commit_to_instead: Agreement signed before departure + go-live plan post-departure

what_never_to_do: Make the offer while a delay signal is unresolved (unless ABROAD with imminent departure)
Offer without a specific expiry date — vague urgency creates no urgency
Make an offer and then extend it more than once — each extension reduces credibility
Close before documenting current provider failures for EX-STL leads — they need to be emotionally ready to leave first
